title: गङ्गा कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गङ्गा कवचम् ॥

अस्य श्री गङ्गा कवच स्तोत्रस्य नारद ऋषिः।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। गङ्गा देवता।

ॐ गङ्गा मे शिरः पातु ललाटं जाह्नवी सदा।
भागीरथी दृशौ पातु श्रोत्रे पातु सुरापगा॥ १॥

नासां मन्दाकिनी पातु मुखं पातु त्रिपथगा।
जिह्वां पातु शिवा देवी कण्ठं पातु विष्णुपदी॥ २॥

स्कन्धौ विश्वनदी पातु बाहू पातु सरिद्वरा।
हस्तौ पुण्यजला पातु वक्षः पातु हरप्रिया॥ ३॥

हृदयं पापनाशिनी उदरं पातु निर्मला।
नाभिं मोक्षदा पातु कटिं पातु जलेश्वरी॥ ४॥

ऊरू देवनदी पातु जानुनी पातु शैलजा।
जङ्घे पातु सुरधुनी पादौ पातु जगन्मही॥ ५॥

प्राच्यां पातु भागीरथी दक्षिणे जाह्नवी सदा।
पश्चिमे मन्दाकिनी च उत्तरे पातु त्रिपथगा॥ ६॥

सर्वाङ्गं पातु मे गङ्गा सर्वतो रक्षतु स्वयम्।
इदं कवचमज्ञात्वा गङ्गास्तोत्रं पठेन्न तु॥ ७॥

यः पठेत् प्रातरुत्थाय गङ्गाकवचमुत्तमम्।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो गङ्गालोकं स गच्छति॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्री गङ्गा कवचम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ganga Kavacham ||

Asya Shri Ganga Kavacha Stotrasya Narada Rishah |
Anushtup Chhandah | Ganga Devata |

Om Ganga Me Shirah Patu Lalatam Jahnavi Sada |
Bhagirathi Drishau Patu Shrotre Patu Surapaga || 1 ||

Nasam Mandakini Patu Mukham Patu Tripathaga |
Jihvam Patu Shiva Devi Kantham Patu Vishnupadi || 2 ||

Skandhau Vishvanadi Patu Bahu Patu Saridvara |
Hastau Punyajala Patu Vakshah Patu Harapriya || 3 ||

Hridayam Papanashini Udaram Patu Nirmala |
Nabhim Mokshada Patu Katim Patu Jaleshvari || 4 ||

Uru Devanadi Patu Januni Patu Shailaja |
Janghe Patu Suradhuni Padau Patu Jaganmahi || 5 ||

Prachyam Patu Bhagirathi Dakshine Jahnavi Sada |
Pashchime Mandakini Cha Uttare Patu Tripathaga || 6 ||

Sarvangam Patu Me Ganga Sarvato Rakshatu Svayam |
Idam Kavacham Ajnatva Gangastotram Pathenna Tu || 7 ||

Yah Pathet Pratarutthaya Ganga Kavacham Uttamam |
Sarvapapavinirmukto Gangalokam Sa Gachchhati || 8 ||

|| Iti Shri Ganga Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
गङ्गा मेरे मस्तक की, जाह्नवी ललाट की, भागीरथी नेत्रों की और सुरापगा कानों की रक्षा करें।

मन्दाकिनी नासिका की, त्रिपथगा मुख की, शिवा जिह्वा की और विष्णुपदी कण्ठ की रक्षा करें।

विश्वनदी कन्धों की, पुण्यजला हाथों की, हरप्रिया वक्ष की रक्षा करें।

पापनाशिनी हृदय की, निर्मला उदर की, मोक्षदा नाभि की और जलेश्वरी कटि की रक्षा करें।

भागीरथी पूर्व में, जाह्नवी दक्षिण में, मन्दाकिनी पश्चिम में और त्रिपथगा उत्तर में रक्षा करें।

जो प्रातःकाल उठकर इस गङ्गा कवच का पाठ करता है, वह सर्वपापमुक्त होकर गङ्गालोक को प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Divine armor of Mother Ganga for complete spiritual protection
Invokes Ganga by 16 sacred names - Jahnavi, Bhagirathi, Mandakini etc.
Invokes purification from all sins and negative karma
Traditionally invoked for protection from all directions with ganga's sacred power
Ideal for those unable to visit Ganga physically
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Ganga, Ganga Kavacham (गङ्गा कवचम्) stands as a divine armor prayer for protection. Attributed to Skanda Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to protect from all directions with Ganga's sacred power as well as grant purification from all sins and negative karma. Additionally, it is known to invoke Ganga by 16 sacred names - Jahnavi, Bhagirathi, Mandakini etc. For best results, devotees chant this on Monday (Somvar) and during Ganga Dussehra. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 4 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Ganga Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ganga Kavacham and what does it invoke?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganga Kavacham (गङ्गा कवचम्) is an eight-verse 'divine armour' (kavacha) hymn invoking Mother Ganga to shield every limb of the body and every direction of space with her purifying, sin-destroying grace. The text is preserved in the Skanda Purana — the largest of the Mahapuranas, which contains extensive Ganga Mahatmya. Its viniyoga declares: Narada is the rishi, Anushtup is the chhanda, and Devi Ganga is the devata. The hymn methodically invokes sixteen sacred names of the Goddess — Ganga, Jahnavi, Bhagirathi, Surapaga, Mandakini, Tripathaga, Shiva, Vishnupadi, Vishvanadi, Saridvara, Punyajala, Harapriya, Papanashini, Nirmala, Mokshada and Jaleshvari — each guarding a specific organ or direction of the body. (Sources: Skanda Purana; classical Ganga upasana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What sixteen names of Mother Ganga appear in this kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganga Kavacham is celebrated for cataloguing sixteen of the most sacred names of the Goddess across its anga-nyasa verses. They are: (1) Ganga — the swift-flowing one; (2) Jahnavi — daughter of Sage Jahnu who released her from his ear; (3) Bhagirathi — brought down by King Bhagiratha's tapasya; (4) Surapaga — river of the gods; (5) Mandakini — the gently-flowing celestial Ganga; (6) Tripathaga — flowing through the three worlds (heaven, earth, netherworld); (7) Shiva Devi — held by Lord Shiva in his jata; (8) Vishnupadi — born from Lord Vishnu's lotus feet (Vamana avatara); (9) Vishvanadi — universal river; (10) Saridvara — the supreme of all rivers; (11) Punyajala — of meritorious water; (12) Harapriya — beloved of Hara; (13) Papanashini — destroyer of all sins; (14) Nirmala — the spotless; (15) Mokshada — bestower of liberation; (16) Jaleshvari — goddess of waters. (Sources: Ganga Kavacham anga-nyasa; Ganga Sahasranama tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Mother Ganga considered to flow in three worlds?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 2 of the Ganga Kavacham invokes the Devi as Tripathaga (त्रिपथगा) — 'the one who flows through three paths.' According to the puranic Ganga-avatarana narrative (preserved in Bhagavata Purana, Ramayana Bala-kanda, Mahabharata and Skanda Purana), King Bhagiratha of the Surya-vamsha undertook severe tapasya to bring the celestial Ganga down to earth to liberate the souls of his ancestors, the 60,000 sons of Sagara, who had been reduced to ashes by the wrath of Sage Kapila. Pleased by his austerities, Brahma released Ganga from the heavens; Lord Shiva caught her descent in his matted locks to prevent her from shattering the earth; and from there she flowed in three streams — Mandakini in svarga, Bhagirathi on earth (following Bhagiratha to the ocean and then down to Patala to liberate the Sagara-putras), and Bhogavati in patala. Hence she is Tripathaga. (Sources: Ramayana Bala-kanda; Bhagavata Purana 9.9; Skanda Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: Which body parts and directions does the Ganga Kavacham protect?
seoFaqs.answer: Following the classical kavacha format, the Ganga Kavacham assigns a Ganga-name to guard each part of the body. Ganga protects the head; Jahnavi the forehead; Bhagirathi the eyes; Surapaga the ears; Mandakini the nose; Tripathaga the mouth; Shiva Devi the tongue; Vishnupadi the throat; Vishvanadi the shoulders; Saridvara the arms; Punyajala the hands; Harapriya the chest; Papanashini the heart; Nirmala the abdomen; Mokshada the navel; Jaleshvari the waist; Devanadi the thighs; Shailaja the knees; Suradhuni the calves; and Jaganmahi the feet. The closing direction-verse seals all four cardinal directions: Bhagirathi in the east, Jahnavi in the south, Mandakini in the west, and Tripathaga in the north. The reciter is therefore enveloped in Ganga-jala from head to toe and from horizon to horizon. (Sources: Ganga Kavacham anga-digbandha verses.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Ganga Kavacham be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganga Kavacham is most powerfully recited on Ganga Dussehra (Jyeshtha Shukla Dashami — the day Ganga descended to earth); on Ganga Saptami (Vaishakha Shukla Saptami — the day Ganga emerged from Brahma's kamandalu); on Ganga Jayanti; on every Mauni Amavasya, Makar Sankranti, Magh Purnima and Kartik Purnima (the four supreme Ganga-snana tithis); on every Mondays (Somvar — sacred to Shiva who holds Ganga); throughout the months of Vaishakha, Magha and Kartik; and during personal pilgrimages to Gangotri, Haridwar, Prayagraj, Kashi and Ganga Sagar. Devotees who cannot physically reach the Ganga recite the Kavacham as a substitute snana — invoking each Ganga-name with bhava is considered to bring the merit of bathing in her waters. The recitation should be done after bath, facing east or north. (Sources: Ganga upasana paddhati; Skanda Purana Ganga Mahatmya.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Ganga Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Ganga Kavacham itself promises — Yah pathet pratarutthaya Ganga-kavacham uttamam, sarva-papa-vinirmukto Ganga-lokam sa gachchhati: 'one who recites this supreme Ganga Kavacham on rising in the morning is freed from all sins and attains Ganga-loka.' Traditional acharyas attribute the following additional benefits: complete bodily protection from accident, illness and injury through Ganga-jala-rakshana of every limb; purification from every species of pataka (cardinal sin) and mahapataka — for Ganga is Papanashini; freedom from samsara through her Mokshada-grace; the merit equivalent to bathing at Gangotri, Haridwar, Prayag, Kashi and Sagara when physical pilgrimage is impossible; relief from pitri-dosha when offered for departed ancestors; and the supreme phala of Ganga-loka — eternal residence in the abode of Mother Ganga, beyond all worldly bondage. (Sources: Ganga Kavacham Phalashruti; Skanda Purana.)
